description: You are analyzing how the NACA 4412 airfoil performs across different Reynolds numbers, which correspond to different operating conditions along a wind turbine blade. Near the root (low speed, large chord), the local Reynolds number is lower; near the tip (high speed, small chord), it is higher. Understanding this variation is critical for accurate blade performance prediction. Perform three XFoil viscous analyses in QBlade using the airfoil coordinate file at /home/ga/Documents/airfoils/naca4412.dat for Reynolds numbers of 200,000, 500,000, and 1,000,000. Use an angle of attack range from -5 to 15 degrees for each analysis. Export the polar datasets to /home/ga/Documents/airfoils/polar_re200k.txt, /home/ga/Documents/airfoils/polar_re500k.txt, and /home/ga/Documents/airfoils/polar_re1m.txt. You are logged in as user 'ga'.
